title: Club Café founder and vice president dies, the venue says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Frank A. Ribaudo, founder of Club Café, died of brain cancer after two-and-a-half-year battle.
Ribaudo transformed Club Café into a hub for Boston's LGBTQ+ community and HIV/AIDS advocacy.
Ribaudo's contributions include advocacy in HIV/AIDS and establishing LGBTQ+ sports support through Club Café.

title: Boston man charged with snatching older women’s purses while riding by them on moped
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Daneuris Diaz, 22, charged with 22 counts including armed robbery and assault targeting older women in Boston.
Charges stem from nine incidents between November 2025 and April 2026, with victims aged 44 to 85.
Diaz identified by booking photos and tattoo, tried to disguise with gloves; surveillance matched clothing to robberies.

title: Norwell issues town-wide boil water order after E. coli detected in water supply
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Norwell issued a town-wide boil water order after E. coli was found in the water supply during routine testing.
Residents must boil tap water for at least one minute before consumption for drinking, cooking, and personal hygiene.
The town is working with the Massachusetts Department of Environmental Protection to resolve the contamination within 48 hours.

title: Willson Contreras to become 1st Red Sox participant in Home Run Derby since 2011
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Willson Contreras will participate in the 2026 Home Run Derby, first Red Sox player since 2011.
Contreras has hit 20 home runs in 88 games for the Red Sox so far this season.
He will also join MLB's All-Star events as a replacement for Vladimir Guerrero Jr., marking his fourth All-Star appearance.

title: Democrat Graham Platner says he plans to withdraw from Maine Senate race after sexual assault claim
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Graham Platner plans to withdraw from the Maine Senate race following a sexual assault allegation.
Platner's withdrawal may deepen divisions between Democratic moderate and progressive factions as the party seeks a replacement.
State law allows Democrats to replace Platner if a new candidate is selected by July 27.

title: Elias Lindholm remains an uncomfortable question mark for retooling Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm has struggled with injuries and performance, impacting his role with the Boston Bruins.
Lindholm was signed to fill the gap left by Patrice Bergeron, but his impact has been limited over two seasons.
Lindholm's future with the Bruins is uncertain due to a large contract and back injuries, complicating the team's strategy.

title: Boston Councilor Julia Mejia suffers medical emergency during City Council meeting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston City Councilor Julia Mejia suffered a medical emergency during a City Council meeting on Wednesday afternoon.
The meeting at City Hall paused for 30 minutes as emergency crews attended to Mejia, who was taken to a hospital.
Mayor Michelle Wu confirmed Mejia is "doing well" and wished her a swift recovery after the incident.

title: My truck’s air conditioner only works when set to full blast
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2007 Ford F-150's AC issue is likely due to a faulty blower motor resistor, costing $20-$65 to replace.
A 2011 Infiniti G25x exhibits sluggish performance post-repair; possible causes include sensor damage or wiring issues.
A 2017 Jeep Compass Trailhawk has battery issues; main and auxiliary batteries may affect the start/stop system.

title: ‘Nine amazing years’: Jayson Tatum on his relationship with Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum described his nine-year partnership with Jaylen Brown at the Celtics as 'amazing', despite media attempts to stir rivalry.
The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and draft picks, reportedly without Tatum's input.
Tatum and Brown won an NBA championship together in 2024 and appeared in two finals during their Boston tenure.

title: 4 standout moments from Tom Brady’s appearance on New Heights
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady appeared on the 'New Heights' podcast discussing his NFL career with hosts Travis and Jason Kelce.
Brady recalled a significant hit from Buffalo's Nate Clements as his 'welcome to the NFL moment.'
Brady described his 2007 season with Randy Moss and his favorite Super Bowl XLIX victory as standout periods.
